prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "胸椎旋转" (thoracic spine rotation) exercise, front-three-quarter view. Person posture: Athletic person performing a thoracic spine rotation in quadruped position: starting on hands and knees (hands under shoulders, knees under hips); right hand placed lightly behind the head with elbow out to the side; rotating the upper back so that the right elbow points up toward the ceiling, opening the chest to the right side; the left arm remains supporting on the ground; lower back and hips remain stable (rotation is from the thoracic spine only, not the lumbar); plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: thoracic spine rotators and obliques. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: rhomboids and serratus anterior of the supporting arm. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "胸椎旋转肌" pointing to the thoracic spine rotators; "腹斜肌" pointing to the obliques; "菱形肌 / 前锯肌" pointing to the rhomboids and serratus anterior. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
